region_of_origin: United States (Brooklyn, New York); sold in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'beer' class (FOODON_03301776); no pilsner-specific subclass exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LN covers beer broadly; no pilsner-specific code available. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 53410008 covers beer as a substance; no pilsner-specific code. Closest parent match used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml bottle of Brooklyn Pilsner, the typical single-serve portion as listed on GrabFood and consistent with standard commercial beer bottle sizing in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beer, regular, all (FDC ID 173190)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Brooklyn Pilsner is a commercial pilsner-style lager. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for regular beer (FDC ID 173190) is used as the closest approved-source equivalent. Pilsner-style beers are a subset of regular lager beer and have comparable macronutrient profiles. Input per_100g values (energy 42.42 kcal, protein 0.45 g, carbohydrate 3.33 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy regular beer values (energy ~43 kcal, protein ~0.46 g, carbohydrate ~3.55 g per 100 g) and are retained with minor rounding. Fat is confirmed zero by product formulation for standard beer. Sodium value of 4.55 mg/100 g is consistent with USDA data (~4 mg/100 g). Adaptation documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Beer (Tennent's lager, UK)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Brooklyn Pilsner. The University of Sydney GI Database and the 2002 International GI Table (Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002;76:5–56) report GI of 66 (medium) for regular lager beer (Tennent's lager, UK). Pilsner-style beers are closely analogous to standard lager in fermentable carbohydrate content and alcohol level. GI of 66 is used as the analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 × 11.0) / 100 = 7.3, where carbohydrate per 330 g serving = 3.33 × 3.30 = 10.99 g ≈ 11.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol raises blood pressure and interacts with antihypertensive medications
hypertension.guidance: Alcohol consumption — even at moderate levels — acutely raises blood pressure and blunts the efficacy of antihypertensive agents including beta-blockers and ACE inhibitors; individuals with hypertension should avoid Brooklyn Pilsner and all alcoholic beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis and malt carbohydrates cause glucose excursions
type2_diabetes.guidance: Alcohol in beer inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis, creating hypoglycaemia risk in patients on insulin or sulfonylureas, while the malt-derived carbohydrates (3.33 g/100 g; ~11 g per bottle) can cause postprandial glucose spikes; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage or consume only under medical supervision and never on an empty stomach.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol is a direct cardiac toxin at regular intake and raises triglycerides
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular alcohol consumption raises serum triglycerides, contributes to cardiomyopathy risk, promotes arrhythmia (holiday heart syndrome), and interacts with anticoagulant and antiplatelet therapies commonly used in cardiovascular disease management; individuals with established CVD should avoid Brooklyn Pilsner.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Alcohol is pro-inflammatory and contraindicated with methotrexate and biologic RA therapies
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Alcohol is directly pro-inflammatory and is contraindicated with methotrexate (increased hepatotoxicity risk) and biologic RA therapies; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid all alcoholic beverages including Brooklyn Pilsner.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Brooklyn Pilsner is a commercial pilsner-style lager beer produced by Brooklyn Brewery (New York, USA) and sold in Singapore via food delivery platforms such as GrabFood in standard 330 ml bottles. Pilsner is a pale, bottom-fermented lager style originating in Bohemia (Czech Republic), characterised by its clear golden colour, moderate bitterness from noble hops, light malt sweetness, and crisp, clean finish with fine carbonation. Brooklyn Pilsner is brewed from water, malted barley, hops, and yeast. It contains approximately 5% alcohol by volume (ABV). Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 42 kcal of energy, 0.45 g protein, negligible fat (confirmed zero by standard beer formulation), and 3.33 g carbohydrate from residual malt sugars. A standard 330 ml bottle delivers approximately 140 kcal and 11 g of carbohydrate. Sodium content is low at approximately 4.6 mg/100 ml. Beer contains gluten from barley malt and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or gluten sensitivity. Clinically, alcohol in beer poses significant risks for individuals with hypertension (raises blood pressure, interacts with antihypertensives), Type 2 Diabetes (hypoglycaemia risk with insulin/sulfonylureas; postprandial glucose excursions from malt carbohydrates), cardiovascular disease (raises triglycerides, arrhythmia risk, drug interactions), and rheumatoid arthritis (contraindicated with methotrexate and biologics). Brooklyn Pilsner is vegetarian and vegan but is not halal or kosher due to its alcohol content. It is commonly paired with burgers, grilled meats, and Western-style dishes in Singapore's casual dining and delivery context.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Brooklyn Pilsner?
Is Brooklyn Pilsner suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink beer if I have hypertension?
How many carbs in a bottle of Brooklyn Pilsner?
What is the alcohol content of Brooklyn Pilsner?
Can I drink beer if I have rheumatoid arthritis?

confidence_reason: Brooklyn Pilsner is a commercial branded beer not individually listed in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy regular beer (FDC ID 173190), which is the closest approved-source equivalent. Input values from GrabFood are consistent with USDA data and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for regular lager beer. Confidence is medium due to the adapted (not exact) source match.
notes: 1. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: fat_g is confirmed zero — standard beer contains no fat by product formulation, consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy beer data. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero by the same formulation basis. sugar_g is confirmed zero — residual fermentable sugars in pilsner-style beer are below detection threshold (trace/confirmed zero per USDA data). dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — beer contains no dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero — beer contains no cholesterol. 2. NUTRIENT ADAPTATION: Brooklyn Pilsner is not individually listed in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved primary source. USDA SR Legacy regular beer (FDC ID 173190) is used as the closest approved-source equivalent. Input per_100g values from GrabFood are consistent with USDA data and are retained. Minor micronutrient values (potassium, calcium, iron, B-vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, selenium, water) are drawn from USDA SR Legacy beer data. 3. GI ESTIMATION: GI of 66 (medium) is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database / Foster-Powell et al. 2002 data for regular lager beer (Tennent's lager). Glycemic load per 330 g serving = (66 × 11.0) / 100 = 7.3. 4. ALCOHOL CONTENT: Brooklyn Pilsner is approximately 5% ABV. Alcohol provides 7 kcal/g and is not captured in standard macronutrient fields; the energy value reflects total caloric contribution including alcohol. 5. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_03301776 (beer), FoodEx2 A00LN (beer), and SNOMED CT 53410008 (beer) are approximate matches; no pilsner-specific codes exist in these ontologies as of 2024. 6. MULTILINGUAL NAMES: Brooklyn Pilsner is a Western commercial brand product sold in Singapore. Although it is not a Singaporean dish by origin, the validator requires mandatory Singaporean multilingual naming coverage (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) for records in the Singapore food database context. Local-language names and aliases have been added accordingly: Chinese Simplified '布鲁克林皮尔森啤酒', Chinese Traditional '布魯克林皮爾森啤酒', Malay 'Bir Pilsner Brooklyn', Tamil 'புரூக்லின் பில்ஸ்னர் பீர்', Hindi 'ब्रुकलिन पिल्सनर बियर'. 7. DIETARY FLAGS: Marked vegetarian and vegan as beer contains no animal-derived ingredients in standard formulation. Not halal or kosher due to alcohol content. Contains gluten from barley malt. 8. ALIASES CONSISTENCY CHECK: All strings in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array — chinese: '布鲁克林皮尔森啤酒', '布魯克林皮爾森啤酒'; malay: 'Bir Pilsner Brooklyn'; tamil: 'புரூக்லின் பில்ஸ்னர் பீர்'; hindi: 'ब्रुकलिन पिल्सनर बियर'; english: all seven entries confirmed present.
